newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9; Aalberts has not issued an update since its FY2025 results cycle and next reports H1 in late summer. The hydronic flow-control / thermal-and-water data-center cooling thesis is unchanged, and the +3.7% move since June 9 is sympathy with the broader DC-cooling/electrification bid rather than a name-specific catalyst.

sources.label: TradingView: Aalberts joins AEX June 22; buyback to date

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold. No fundamental change since June 9 on the thermal/water hidden-bottleneck lens; AEX index inclusion (June 22) is a passive-flow event, not new alpha. Hydronic/data-centre cooling exposure intact but indirect; +3.7% drift is noise.
verdicts.bull: Hydronic flow control and thermal/surface tech indirectly levered to data-centre liquid cooling at a non-AI multiple; margins expanding.
verdicts.neutral: Diversified industrial; AI exposure real but small and indirect. Index inclusion does not change the thesis.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical residential/construction drag; AI-cooling link is thin and speculative for a core bottleneck case.
